motto: Known covert programs, sub-projects, and obscure tags — merge-only dossier tagging with location pins.

programs.label: MKUltra
programs.description: CIA program on behavior modification and mind control using LSD, hypnosis, sensory deprivation, electroshock, and abuse. 149 subprojects; most records destroyed 1973; Church Committee exposed surviving files.

programs.label: Project Monarch
programs.description: Alleged MKUltra subproject involving trauma-based dissociation and programmed personalities. Not confirmed in declassified CIA inventories; widely cited in survivor testimony and conspiracy literature. Tag for cross-reference only.

programs.label: Project Artichoke
programs.description: CIA precursor to MKUltra focused on offensive interrogation, hypnosis, and forced drug use. Predecessor to Bluebird; merged into MKUltra umbrella.

programs.label: Project Bluebird
programs.description: Early CIA mind-control initiative (1950) on hypnosis, drugs, and personality alteration. Renamed Artichoke then absorbed into MKUltra.

programs.label: Project Chatter
programs.description: US Navy program predating CIA efforts — truth serums and interrogation drugs. Overlapped with early CIA mind-control research.

programs.label: Project OFTEN / CHICKWIT
programs.description: MKUltra-linked projects studying foreign pharmaceutical mind-altering compounds and parapsychology applications.

programs.label: Operation Midnight Climax
programs.description: MKUltra subproject using San Francisco and New York safehouses to dose unwitting subjects with LSD while CIA observers watched behind one-way glass.

programs.label: MKNAOMI
programs.description: CIA–Army joint program on biological agents, toxins, and delivery systems for covert operations. Sister program to MKUltra under Technical Services Division.

programs.label: MKDELTA
programs.description: CIA designation for mind-control and chemical interrogation work conducted overseas on unwitting foreign nationals.

programs.label: MKSEARCH
programs.description: Successor codename consolidating remaining MKUltra subprojects after scale-back; focused on behavior influence and incapacitating agents.

programs.label: Allan Memorial Institute
programs.description: McGill University psychiatric hospital where Dr. Ewen Cameron ran CIA-funded 'psychic driving' and depatterning experiments — electroshock, drug coma, sensory isolation.

programs.label: Operation Paperclip
programs.description: US program recruiting German scientists and intelligence specialists after WWII — including von Braun and former Nazi researchers — into US military and NASA programs.

programs.label: Operation Mockingbird
programs.description: CIA influence and asset recruitment within US and foreign media organizations for propaganda and intelligence placement. Church Committee documented CIA–media relationships.

programs.label: COINTELPRO
programs.description: FBI covert program to surveil, infiltrate, discredit, and disrupt domestic political organizations — including civil rights, anti-war, and Black liberation groups.

programs.label: Operation Northwoods
programs.description: Pentagon/JCS proposal (never executed) for staged terrorist acts and hijackings blamed on Cuba to justify invasion. Declassified 1997.

programs.label: Project Stargate
programs.description: DIA/CIA-funded remote viewing and psychic espionage program. Multiple codenames over decades; declassified with mixed claimed operational results.

programs.label: Project Grill Flame
programs.description: Army/DIA remote-viewing unit later folded into Stargate. Personnel conducted thousands of sessions on intelligence targets.

programs.label: Phoenix Program
programs.description: CIA–MACV counterinsurgency program in Vietnam using capture, interrogation, assassination, and informant networks against Viet Cong infrastructure.

programs.label: Tuskegee Syphilis Study
programs.description: US Public Health Service study withholding treatment from Black men with syphilis to observe disease progression. Exposed 1972; led to Belmont Report reforms.

programs.label: Project SHAD
programs.description: DoD program exposing US sailors to chemical and biological agents without informed consent to test ship vulnerability and decontamination.

programs.label: Operation CHAOS
programs.description: CIA illegal domestic surveillance of anti-war and student movements. Exposed by investigative journalists and Church Committee.

programs.label: MKUltra Subproject 68
programs.description: Specific MKUltra funding line for Cameron's depatterning research at Allan Memorial Institute, Montreal.

programs.label: Manhattan Project
programs.description: WWII secret program to develop nuclear weapons. Compartmentalized sites: Los Alamos, Oak Ridge, Hanford, and dozens of obscure codename facilities.

programs.label: Montauk Project
programs.description: Alleged secret experiments at Camp Hero/Montauk Air Force Station involving mind control, time travel, and psychic weapons. No official documentation; popularized by Preston Nichols books.

programs.label: Philadelphia Experiment
programs.description: Urban legend of Navy invisibility/teleportation experiment on USS Eldridge. Navy denies; no credible declassified record.

programs.label: HAARP
aliases: High Frequency Active Auroral Research Program
programs.description: Ionospheric research facility in Alaska (DARPA/USAF/University of Alaska). Subject of weather-control and mind-control conspiracy theories; peer-reviewed literature describes radio science use.

programs.label: Operation Gladio
programs.description: NATO/CIA-backed clandestine stay-behind armies in Europe intended for resistance after Soviet invasion; linked to false-flag violence in Italy and other nations.

programs.label: Operation 40
programs.description: CIA covert action group formed for Bay of Pigs; included future figures in Iran-Contra and assassination conspiracy literature.

programs.label: MKUltra media manipulation
programs.description: MKUltra lines exploring electromagnetic and sensory influences on human behavior and potential media-broadcast applications.

programs.label: Project Pandora
programs.description: DoD study of microwave radiation effects after US embassy Moscow beam incidents; examined behavioral and health impacts of RF exposure.

programs.label: Jonestown / Peoples Temple
programs.description: Mass death event in Guyana. Documented CIA interest in Peoples Temple leader Jim Jones and State Department cables; extent of agency involvement remains debated in FOIA releases.

programs.label: Operation Earnest Voice
programs.description: US government software and programs for online persona management and foreign influence operations; publicly discussed in defense contracting literature.

programs.label: Covert program (generic tag)
programs.description: Operator-defined tag for undocumented or locally known covert programs. Pair with precise location and notes.
